heading: Eat directly from the comb
content:
The most traditional way to enjoy Sundarbans Raw Comb Honey is to cut a small piece of honeycomb and eat it directly. The honeycomb cells release honey naturally when chewed.

* Enjoy as a natural snack
* Consume honey and comb together
* Chew the wax and swallow or discard according to personal preference

heading: As a premium breakfast topping
content:
Raw comb honey pairs well with:

* Toast and bread
* Paratha or ruti
* Pancakes and waffles
* Oatmeal and porridge
* Yogurt bowls
* Fresh fruits

heading: In beverages
content:
The honey portion can be mixed into:

* Warm water
* Herbal tea
* Black tea
* Lemon water
* Ginger drinks

For best flavor and preservation of natural properties, add honey after the beverage has cooled to a warm, drinkable temperature.

heading: As a gourmet serving item
content:
Comb honey is often served on:

* Cheese boards
* Dessert platters
* Fruit trays
* Special occasion breakfast spreads

Its natural honeycomb structure makes it visually appealing as well as edible.

</section>

heading: General guidance
content:
* Consume in moderation as part of a balanced diet.
* Honey is a natural sweetener and contains naturally occurring sugars.
* Serving size can be adjusted according to personal dietary needs and preferences.

</section>

heading: Honeycomb breakfast platter
content:
Serve small comb sections with:

* Fresh fruits
* Yogurt
* Nuts
* Toast

A simple way to enjoy the natural flavor of the honey.

heading: Warm lemon honey drink
content:
Ingredients:

* 1 cup warm water
* 1 teaspoon honey from the comb
* Fresh lemon juice

Mix and serve warm.

heading: Yogurt and honeycomb bowl
content:
Ingredients:

* Plain yogurt
* Small pieces of honeycomb
* Seasonal fruits
* Chopped nuts

Top the yogurt with fruit and honeycomb for added sweetness and texture.

heading: Cheese and comb honey board
content:
Pair small honeycomb pieces with:

* Mild cheeses
* Crackers
* Fresh grapes
* Nuts

A popular serving style for gatherings and special occasions.

heading: Honey-drizzled fruit salad
content:
Drizzle honey extracted from the comb over:

* Apples
* Bananas
* Papaya
* Berries
* Citrus fruits

Serve immediately.

heading: Natural dessert topping
content:
Use the honey portion as a topping for:

* Ice cream
* Pancakes
* Waffles
* Rice pudding
* Homemade desserts

</section>

heading: Avoid very high heat
content:
Excessive heating can alter the flavor, aroma, and natural characteristics of raw honey.

Avoid:

* Boiling honey
* Cooking at prolonged high temperatures
* Adding directly to extremely hot liquids

heading: Do not feed to infants under 12 months
heading_path: Do not feed to infants under 12 months
content: Honey should not be given to babies younger than one year of age.

heading: Avoid contamination
content:
To maintain quality:

* Use clean, dry utensils
* Keep the container tightly sealed
* Prevent water from entering the container

heading: Do not refrigerate unnecessarily
content: Refrigeration may accelerate crystallization and make the honey more difficult to use. Store at normal room temperature in a cool, dry place.

heading: Do not mistake crystallization for spoilage
heading_path: Do not mistake crystallization for spoilage
content: Natural crystallization is common in pure honey and does not indicate that the product has gone bad. If desired, place the container in mildly warm water to soften the honey.

heading: Be aware of bee-product sensitivities
content:
Individuals with known allergies or sensitivities to bee products, pollen, or related substances should exercise caution before consuming comb honey.

</section>
